Image by AuthorThe Interview Question
You’re interviewing for a staff-level ML systems role. The interviewer asks:
Your agent runs everything on GPT-4o ($15/MTok). You add a routing layer that sends easy calls to GPT-4o mini ($0.15/MTok) — a 100x cheaper model. The router works perfectly, but your bill doesn’t drop. Why?
Most candidates answer: “The routing logic has latency overhead” or “The cheap model makes mistakes.”
Both wrong.
The real answer? Prompt caching destroyed your savings.
Model routing is usually explained as a three-step pipeline:
Incoming Request
│
▼
Route Request
│
┌──────┴────────┐
▼ ▼
Cheap LLM Premium LLMThe intuition seems obvious:

• Easy tasks → cheap model

• Hard tasks → expensive model

• Lower average cost
For single-turn chat applications, this often works.
Production agents, however, are very different.
Agent Tasks Are Not Single LLM Calls
